title: Pilot 002: Executive Briefing
productQuestion: Can VALOUR help a technical leader convert a complex technology recommendation into a concise executive decision briefing?
scenario: A technical leader needs to brief senior executives on a complex technology decision. The risk is going too deep into technical detail, losing executive attention, and failing to connect the recommendation to business risk, cost, timing, and decision options.

stages.label: Intake
stages.summary: Captures the technical leader role, executive briefing challenge, starting confidence, desired outcome, concern, and selected scenario.

stages.label: Scenario Selection
stages.summary: Confirms Executive Briefing as the correct scenario because the user must compress technical judgement into business decision language.

stages.label: Preparation
stages.summary: Defines executive audience, stakeholders, decision required, context, business risk, likely pushback, response options, opening frame, and close.

stages.label: Rehearsal
stages.summary: Runs simulated executive challenge questions and improves technical draft answers into concise business-focused responses.

stages.label: Language Refinement
stages.summary: Converts detailed technical briefing language into executive, business, trade-off, and decision-oriented language.

stages.label: After-Action Review
stages.summary: Records what happened, what worked, where clarity improved, where composure was tested, feedback received, and ending confidence.

stages.label: Pattern Report
stages.summary: Identifies technical detail before executive relevance as the leadership pattern, plus strength, risk, scenario fit, development focus, and coaching note.

stages.label: Evidence Record
stages.summary: Summarises product evidence, confidence shift, observed improvement, product learning, remaining weakness, recommendation, and repeat decision.
